computation_name: Pivot, Permutation, and Ptolemaic Metric Indexing
definition: Use distances to pivots, permutation signatures, or Ptolemaic inequalities to prune expensive metric-distance comparisons. Candidate pairs are then verified with the exact metric before converting distance to SPECTRA similarity.

scale_profile.best_for: Structured scientific objects with an expensive metric distance and no natural vector coordinates.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large collections when metric pruning is effective and intrinsic dimension is moderate.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Distance concentration makes pivot pruning collapse to near-linear scan.

tunable_parameters.name: pivot_count
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls pruning power and preprocessing cost.

tunable_parameters.name: pivot_selection_rule
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls whether pivots cover the metric space effectively.

quality_gates: Verify exact distances for all emitted candidates.
Report pivot selection and candidate-pruning statistics.
Measure recall or exactness on a brute-force subset.
failure_modes: High intrinsic dimension can make pivot indexes ineffective.
Bad pivot choice can miss useful pruning opportunities.
Non-metric distances invalidate triangle- or Ptolemy-based pruning.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py only as an output-contract reference; production pivot indexes should verify exact metric distances and emit the same SPECTRA columns.

citations.title: Indexing Metric Spaces for Exact Similarity Search
citations.evidence: Surveys and compares metric indexing techniques for exact similarity search across data distributions.

citations.title: Permutation Search Methods are Efficient, Yet Faster Search is Possible
citations.evidence: Studies permutation-based methods for approximate k-nearest-neighbor search in metric spaces.
